quiz.title: SAT Mathematics: Unit 9 Quiz 3
quiz.subtitle: Units and Unit Conversion
quiz.subject: SAT Preparation
quiz.topic: Converting between units in real-world contexts

questions.question: A landscaper needs to cover a rectangular garden that measures 12 feet by 15 feet with mulch. If the mulch is sold only by the square yard, how many square yards of mulch are needed to cover the garden? (3 feet = 1 yard)
questions.explanation: First, find the area in square feet: $12 \text{ ft} \times 15 \text{ ft} = 180 \text{ sq ft}$. Since $1 \text{ yd} = 3 \text{ ft}$, $1 \text{ sq yd} = (3 \text{ ft})^2 = 9 \text{ sq ft}$. To convert to square yards, divide the total square footage by 9: $180 / 9 = 20 \text{ sq yds}$.

questions.question: A marathon runner maintains a constant speed of 4 meters per second. Which of the following is closest to the runner's speed in kilometers per hour? (1 kilometer = 1,000 meters)
questions.explanation: Convert meters per second to meters per hour: $4 \text{ m/s} \times 60 \text{ s/min} \times 60 \text{ min/hr} = 14{,}400 \text{ meters per hour}$. Convert meters to kilometers: $14{,}400 / 1{,}000 = 14.4 \text{ km/hr}$.

questions.question: A certain brand of paint covers 400 square feet per gallon. A painter is painting the walls of a room with a total surface area of 6,000 square inches. How many gallons of paint are needed to cover the walls once? (1 foot = 12 inches)
questions.explanation: Convert the surface area from square inches to square feet. Since $1 \text{ sq ft} = 12 \text{ in} \times 12 \text{ in} = 144 \text{ sq in}$, the area in square feet is $6{,}000 / 144 \approx 41.67 \text{ sq ft}$. Since one gallon covers 400 sq ft, the amount of paint needed is $41.67 / 400 \approx 0.104 \text{ gallons}$.

questions.question: A fuel tank has a capacity of 50 liters. If fuel is being pumped into the tank at a rate of 250 milliliters per second, how many minutes will it take to fill the empty tank? (1 liter = 1,000 milliliters)
questions.explanation: Total capacity in milliliters: $50 \text{ L} \times 1{,}000 = 50{,}000 \text{ mL}$. Time in seconds: $50{,}000 \text{ mL} / 250 \text{ mL/s} = 200 \text{ seconds}$. Convert seconds to minutes: $200 / 60 \approx 3.33 \text{ minutes}$.

questions.question: A heavy-duty truck can carry a maximum load of 2.5 tons. If a construction company needs to transport crates that weigh 400 pounds each, what is the maximum number of crates the truck can carry in a single trip? (1 ton = 2,000 pounds)
questions.explanation: Total capacity in pounds: $2.5 \text{ tons} \times 2{,}000 \text{ lbs/ton} = 5{,}000 \text{ lbs}$. Divide by the weight of one crate: $5{,}000 / 400 = 12.5$. Since the truck cannot carry a partial crate that exceeds its weight limit, the maximum whole number is 12.

questions.question: A certain brand of garden hose delivers water at a rate of 10 gallons per minute. A worker is using the hose to fill a pool with a total capacity of 300 cubic feet. Which of the following is closest to the number of hours it will take to fill the pool? (1 cubic foot ≈ 7.5 gallons)
questions.explanation: First, convert the pool's capacity from cubic feet to gallons: $300 \times 7.5 = 2{,}250$ gallons. Next, find the time in minutes: $2{,}250 / 10 = 225$ minutes. Convert minutes to hours: $225 / 60 = 3.75$ hours.

questions.question: The speed limit on a particular highway in Europe is 110 kilometers per hour. Which of the following is closest to the speed limit in miles per hour? (1 mile ≈ 1.61 kilometers)
questions.explanation: To convert from kilometers per hour to miles per hour, divide the speed by the conversion factor: $110 / 1.61 \approx 68.32$ miles per hour. Choice B is the closest.

questions.question: A scientist measures the mass of a chemical sample to be 0.045 kilograms. What is the mass of the sample in milligrams?
questions.explanation: Convert kilograms to grams: $0.045 \times 1{,}000 = 45$ grams. Convert grams to milligrams: $45 \times 1{,}000 = 45{,}000$ milligrams.

questions.question: A rectangular solar panel has dimensions of 1.5 meters by 2.0 meters. What is the area of the solar panel in square centimeters? (1 meter = 100 centimeters)
questions.explanation: First, find the area in square meters: $1.5 \times 2.0 = 3$ square meters. Since $1 \text{ meter} = 100 \text{ cm}$, $1 \text{ square meter} = (100)^2 = 10{,}000$ square centimeters. Therefore, $3 \times 10{,}000 = 30{,}000$ square centimeters.

questions.question: A recipe for a large batch of soup calls for 4.5 liters of broth. If a chef only has a measuring cup that measures in fluid ounces, approximately how many fluid ounces of broth are needed? (1 liter ≈ 33.8 fluid ounces)
questions.explanation: Multiply the number of liters by the conversion factor: $4.5 \times 33.8 = 152.1$ fluid ounces.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent work! You can confidently convert between units across systems, handle area/volume conversions correctly, and apply dimensional analysis to real-world problems.
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review missed questions and focus on remembering that area conversions require squaring the linear factor (e.g., $3 \text{ ft} = 1 \text{ yd}$, but $9 \text{ sq ft} = 1 \text{ sq yd}$).
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Remember: always write out your conversion factors as fractions so units cancel correctly, and double-check whether you're converting linear, area, or volume measurements.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Start by memorizing common conversion factors, practice setting up dimensional analysis chains, and always ask: 'Am I converting length, area, or volume?'
